results.title: The Magic pear tree
results.creator_credit: Wan-go Wen, Alfred Wallace and Film Dimensions
results.brief_description: A tale about a pear peddler who is greedy and selfish, a poor old man who couldn’t afford to buy a pear, and a kind passer-by who buys a pear for the old man. Points out that we should have compassion for the poor and respect for the elderly.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Wan-go Wen, Alfred Wallace and Film Dimensions
results.description: A tale about a pear peddler who is greedy and selfish, a poor old man who couldn’t afford to buy a pear, and a kind passer-by who buys a pear for the old man. Points out that we should have compassion for the poor and respect for the elderly.

results.title: The Dragon's tears
results.creator_credit: John Korty, Contemproary Films and McGraw-Hill Films
results.brief_description: An animated adaptation of Hirosuke Hamada’s story of a little boy’s fearless act of inviting a dragon to his birthday party. Utilizes Japanese-style watercolour and art work. Narrated by Robert Morse. Suitable for lower and middle primary levels.
results.creator_credit_for_label: John Korty, Contemproary Films and McGraw-Hill Films

results.title: The Stones in the park
results.brief_description: The phenomenon of the rock concert is explored in this film of the Rolling Stones concert in Hyde Park, London 1969, a few days after the death Brian Jones. Includes interviews and behind-the-scenes footage. Photographed by Bill Brayne, Michael Dudds and Brian Grainger.
results.description: The phenomenon of the rock concert is explored in this film of the Rolling Stones concert in Hyde Park, London 1969, a few days after the death Brian Jones. Includes interviews and behind-the-scenes footage. Photographed by Bill Brayne, Michael Dudds and Brian Grainger.

results.title: Mechanisms of non-verbal communication in the young child
results.brief_description: Looks at ways in which young children aged from eighteen months to three years communicate with each other, and shows the influence of the home and the mother on the behaviour patterns of children in relation to others.

results.title: A Sense of movement
results.creator_credit: Sue Jerrard, Veronica Sherborne and University of Bristol
results.brief_description: Made at New Fosseway School for severely subnormal children in Bristol, England, the film shows 15 year olds and 6 year olds taking movement classes with Veronica Sherborne.

results.title: Over-the-counter pills and promises
results.creator_credit: Lee Rhoads, Alfred Higgins and Alfred Higgins Productions
results.brief_description: Shows that highly advertised over-the-counter medicines may not offer the best values and that less expensive brands and home remedies are often just as effective.

results.title: Hope is not a method. - Rev. ed.
results.brief_description: Cites and describes seven contraceptive methods with anatomiclaly explicit diagrams. States the advantages and disadvantages of withdrawal, rhythm, spermicidal chemicals, the condom, the diaphragm, the pill, the IUD and sterilization. Suitable for upper secondary level.

results.title: Exponents: a study in generalisation
results.creator_credit: Ray Redheffer, Stephen Slocomb, Charles Eames and Ray Eames
results.brief_description: Animated symbols are used to explain the powers of a number and the use of the exponent in expanded notation. This film has no dialogue and relies entirely on animated visual illustrations to define exponents.

results.title: The Consumer game
results.creator_credit: Noel Nosseck, Directions Unlimited and Pyramid Films
results.brief_description: Reviews psychological and motivational techniques used to sell a product. Explains the concepts of comparison shopping, unit pricing, convenient packaging, financing, guarantees and labels. Hosted by Artie Johnston.
